---
title: "Sphinx Documentation Build"
subtitle: "Verifying HTML autodoc and Word export"
date: today
date-format: "DD MMMM YYYY HH:mm"
---

## What this page covers

Issues [#15](https://github.com/almac09/private-rag-assistant/issues/15) and
[#16](https://github.com/almac09/private-rag-assistant/issues/16) verify that the
`rag/` Python module can be built into readable documentation using Sphinx, and that
a Word version of the project report can be exported for submission.

---

## HTML build — Sphinx autodoc

Sphinx reads the docstrings in `rag/ingest.py`, `rag/query.py`, `rag/evaluate.py`,
and `rag/logger.py` and renders them as cross-linked HTML documentation.

**How to build:**

```bash
sphinx-build -b html docs/_source docs/_build/html
```

**Output:** `docs/_build/html/index.html`

The build uses:

| Extension | Purpose |
|-----------|---------|
| `myst_parser` | Parses Markdown source files (`.md`) |
| `sphinx.ext.autodoc` | Pulls docstrings from Python modules |
| `sphinx.ext.viewcode` | Adds "view source" links to each function |
| `sphinx_rtd_theme` | Read the Docs visual theme |

**Install dependencies:**

```bash
uv sync   # myst-parser and sphinx-rtd-theme are in pyproject.toml
```

---

## Word export — Quarto render

The Sphinx ecosystem does not have a maintained docx builder compatible with
current Sphinx versions. The project report (`quarto/report.qmd`) is exported
to Word via Quarto, which uses Pandoc internally and produces clean, fully
formatted Word documents.

**How to build:**

```bash
quarto render quarto/report.qmd --to docx --output-dir docs/_build
```

**Output:** `docs/_build/report.docx`

This is also available as **VS Code Task 10 — Quarto Render Report (docx)**.

---

## Autodoc output preview

```{python}
#| code-fold: false
import inspect
import rag.query as q

# Show the public API extracted by sphinx autodoc
public_fns = [(name, obj) for name, obj in inspect.getmembers(q, inspect.isfunction)
              if not name.startswith("_")]

for name, fn in public_fns:
    sig = inspect.signature(fn)
    doc_first_line = (inspect.getdoc(fn) or "").split("\n")[0]
    print(f"rag.query.{name}{sig}")
    print(f"  {doc_first_line}")
    print()
```

```{python}
import rag.logger as lg

public_fns = [(name, obj) for name, obj in inspect.getmembers(lg, inspect.isfunction)
              if not name.startswith("_")]

for name, fn in public_fns:
    sig = inspect.signature(fn)
    doc_first_line = (inspect.getdoc(fn) or "").split("\n")[0]
    print(f"rag.logger.{name}{sig}")
    print(f"  {doc_first_line}")
    print()
```

---

## Build status

```{python}
import subprocess, sys

def check_sphinx():
    result = subprocess.run(
        [sys.executable, "-c", "import sphinx; print(sphinx.__version__)"],
        capture_output=True, text=True
    )
    return result.stdout.strip() if result.returncode == 0 else "not installed"

def check_myst():
    result = subprocess.run(
        [sys.executable, "-c", "import myst_parser; print(myst_parser.__version__)"],
        capture_output=True, text=True
    )
    return result.stdout.strip() if result.returncode == 0 else "not installed"

print(f"Sphinx version   : {check_sphinx()}")
print(f"myst-parser      : {check_myst()}")
print(f"Output directory : docs/_build/html/")
print(f"Word export      : docs/_build/report.docx")
print(f"Build command    : sphinx-build -b html docs/_source docs/_build/html")
```

---

## Note on Sphinx docx builders

The `sphinxcontrib-docxbuilder` package listed in the original `pyproject.toml`
does not exist on PyPI. The alternative `sphinx-docxbuilder` package (2019) is
incompatible with Sphinx 7+. Word export is therefore handled by Quarto/Pandoc,
which produces better output and requires no additional dependencies beyond Quarto
itself.

The `sphinxcontrib-docxbuilder` entry has been removed from `pyproject.toml` and
from `docs/_source/conf.py`. The VS Code task has been updated accordingly.
